pattern: The cheapest way to cover a prefix only depends on the cheapest way to cover a shorter prefix plus the cost of one new final segment, which is a classic left-to-right dynamic program.
hints: Think of it as covering the array left to right: the best cost to cover the first i elements is the best cost to cover some earlier prefix plus the cost of the segment that finishes at i.
For a fixed right end, extending the segment's left boundary one step at a time lets you update how many values now repeat at least twice without rescanning the whole segment.

Track, for each segment length change, how many elements just became a first repeat (contributing two to the segment's cost) versus an additional repeat (contributing one more) as the left boundary moves.

edge_inputs.description: a mix of repeated and unique values
a large fixed cost with a short array of repeats
no value ever repeats, so one single segment is always best
a large fixed cost favors keeping everything in one segment despite repeats
a small fixed cost favors splitting into many tiny segments instead of paying for repeats
one value repeated across a slightly larger array

stress.description: a large array with values reused often enough to create real splitting trade-offs
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 1000))
    nums = [rng.randint(0, max(0, n - 1)) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randint(1, 1000000000)
    return [nums, k]
